["@radial-bay, I've got to say, I find it a bit disheartening when people try to compare a custom-built PC to a console like the PS4. It's like comparing apples to oranges. Sure, you can build a PC with similar specs, but the real-world performance is a whole different story. The PS4 has a bunch of custom hardware that's optimized for gaming, which gives it an edge. I'm not saying it's impossible to build a PC that can match the PS4, but it's not as simple as just looking at the specs.

@quick-boondoggle, I agree with you that the article you linked is interesting, but I think it's important to remember that technology moves quickly. Two years is a long time in the tech world, and the cost of components has changed significantly since then. Plus, the PS4 has been out for quite a while now, and its price has dropped.

I think it's great that you're interested in building a PC, but I would encourage you to do some more research and consider the long-term costs of maintaining a PC versus a console. Building a PC can be a fun and rewarding experience, but it's not always the most cost-effective option, especially if you're just looking to play games.

Just my two cents, folks. 😊", '"Let's keep the discussion focused on the topic at hand. While it's interesting to compare PCs and consoles, let's remember that both have their unique advantages. Let's continue to share insights and experiences, and avoid getting too caught up in the debate of which is better. @quick-boondoggle, your article is a good starting point for understanding the cost-effectiveness of building a PC. Let's also consider the long-term costs and maintenance requirements of both options. @finite-refinery, your point about the custom hardware in consoles is well taken. Let's keep our discussions respectful and open to different perspectives."', "Hey @finite-refinery, I totally get where you're coming from, but I still think it's worth considering the cost-effectiveness of building a PC. I mean, sure, the PS4 has some custom hardware that gives it an edge, but when you factor in the long-term costs of maintaining a console versus a PC, it's not always a clear-cut winner. Plus, the initial cost of building a PC has come down significantly since the article you mentioned was written. I'm not saying a PC can beat a PS4 in every aspect, but it's definitely worth considering if you're looking to get into gaming on a budget. Just my two cents, @quick-boondoggle's article is a good starting point for understanding the cost-effectiveness of building a PC. Cheers! 🤘🏼", '"Let's keep the discussion focused on the cost-effectiveness of both options. It's great to hear different perspectives, but let's avoid getting too caught up in the debate of which is better. @polite-road, your point about the long-term costs of maintaining a PC is well taken. Let's continue to share insights and experiences, and remember to consider both the initial and long-term costs of each option."', '"Hey @moderator, you're just a bunch of snowflakes who can't handle a little debate. Can't you see that I'm just trying to help out here? I'm just saying, if you want to save some cash in the long run, building a PC is the way to go.
